commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 32. "For who is God, but the Lord?" (ver. 31) whom we serve. "And who God, but our God?" And who is God, but the Lord? whom after good service we sons shall possess as the hoped-for inheritance.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Our God. Will any one then hinder Him from doing as He has said? (Haydock)
Hebrew, "Who is the rock but our God? "(Berthier)
God is often styled a rock, tsur. Yet St. Jerome and Pagnin render it "strong "or "the strong one. "(Haydock)
There is only one Lord and Saviour of all. (Worthington)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
